subject: a getaway driver's last job
lyrics:
[Verse]
Engine warm at a quarter past two
Meter running on a debt long due
They said one more and then I'm clean
Fastest wheels this town has seen

[Verse]
Three sharp knocks and they pile inside
Duffel bags and a nervous ride
Green light flicker, I count to four
Never look at what they're running for

[Chorus]
One last job, then I'm gone
Leave the radio playing my favorite song
Foot on the gas, heart in my throat
This is the line where I learn to float

[Verse]
Sirens braiding through the night
I know every alley, every light
Gotta retirement out past the bay
If these tires can hold their grip today

[Bridge]
Brass section blasting in my chest
Never folded, never confessed
But a man can only thread the eye
So many times before he says goodbye

[Chorus]
One last job, then I'm gone
Leave the radio playing my favorite song
Foot on the gas, heart in my throat
This is the line where I learn to float

subject: the morning after a funeral
lyrics:
[Verse]
Neon fading into gray
Coffee cold from yesterday
Black suit hanging on the door
Don't know what it's for anymore

[Verse]
Cars go by like nothing changed
Everything I knew rearranged
Sunrise bleeds through plastic blinds
Leaving you a hundred times

[Chorus]
The morning after, the city hums
A hollow beat where your heart once drummed
I hit the highway, I let it glide
With all your ghost lights on my side

[Verse]
Phone still holds your final call
I play it back against the wall
Voice all static, soft and worn
The last thing left of you reborn

[Bridge]
Synths rise up like distant rain
Washing out the dial tone pain
I'll carry you in chrome and light
A passenger through every night

[Chorus]
The morning after, the city hums
A hollow beat where your heart once drummed
I hit the highway, I let it glide
With all your ghost lights on my side

subject: a factory closing down
lyrics:
[Verse]
Last shift whistle, nobody cheers
Forty-three of the longest years
Lockers empty, the lights stay on
A little while after we're all gone

[Verse]
My old man clocked these same gray floors
Now they're chaining shut the doors
A town that ran on iron and sweat
Learning words we don't know yet

[Chorus]
The machines go quiet, the smoke goes clear
What do you do when there's nothing here
We built this place with our own two hands
Now it's blueprints in a stranger's plans

[Verse]
Hank's gonna drive a truck out west
Maria says she'll do her best
The diner's emptier every day
Like the whole street's slipping away

[Bridge]
We were never just a line on a sheet
We were Friday nights on a one-stop street
We were proud and we were paid
Look at everything we made

[Chorus]
The machines go quiet, the smoke goes clear
What do you do when there's nothing here
We built this place with our own two hands
Now it's blueprints in a stranger's plans

subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
lyrics:
[Verse]
Flat horizon, a bruise of green
The widest sky I've ever seen
I follow the pressure dropping low
Toward the only home I know

[Chorus]
I can't stay still, I chase the gray
Where the funnel writes and tears away
Something in me needs the roar
A quiet life I can't endure

[Verse]
Motel keys and a tank near dry
Maps unfolding across the sky
She asked me once to put down roots
I watched the wind take off my boots

[Bridge]
In the eye there's a kind of peace
Where the spinning lets the silence release
Maybe that's all I'm chasing for
The stillness at the center of the storm

[Chorus]
I can't stay still, I chase the gray
Where the funnel writes and tears away
Something in me needs the roar
A quiet life I can't endure

subject: a love that ended over a text message
lyrics:
[Verse]
Three gray dots and then they're gone
The screen goes dark, the night drags on
No slammed door, no raised voice
Just a sentence and a final choice

[Chorus]
You ended us in seven words
No melody, no singing birds
Just the soft blue glow of goodbye
A whisper too small to make me cry

[Verse]
I read it back a thousand times
Looking for the hidden rhymes
A reason folded in the lines
But all I find is empty signs

[Bridge]
We could have used a slow last song
A room, a touch, a goodnight long
Instead the silence learns to stay
In the space where your name used to lay

[Chorus]
You ended us in seven words
No melody, no singing birds
Just the soft blue glow of goodbye
A whisper too small to make me cry

subject: running a pirate radio station
lyrics:
[Verse]
Up on the rooftop, antenna high
Throwing our signal across the sky
The man says hush but we say swing
Listen close to the bell I ring

[Chorus]
We're broadcasting trouble on a borrowed wave
A midnight band and a beat to save
Dial us in if you're feeling bold
This is the frequency they can't hold

[Verse]
Mabel on the mic with a velvet tone
Reading dedications to the night alone
A trumpet wails through the static hiss
No license needed for a kiss like this

[Bridge]
They trace the tower, they cut the line
We pack the records, we're doing fine
Tomorrow night from a different roof
The music's the only living proof

[Chorus]
We're broadcasting trouble on a borrowed wave
A midnight band and a beat to save
Dial us in if you're feeling bold
This is the frequency they can't hold

subject: two rivals forced to dance together
lyrics:
[Verse]
They paired us up, what a cruel little joke
You rolled your eyes and I nearly choked
Hand on your waist like a loaded gun
Counting the seconds till this is done

[Chorus]
One two three and we move as one
Hating the way that it's kind of fun
Enemies caught in a borrowed song
Proving the both of us wrong

[Verse]
You step on my foot, I step on your pride
But the rhythm keeps pulling us side to side
The crowd disappears and the lights go warm
Two old rivals in the eye of the storm

[Bridge]
Maybe the fight was a kind of dance
Maybe we never gave it a chance
Your hand fits mine like it always could
I'd lose to you if it felt this good

[Chorus]
One two three and we move as one
Hating the way that it's kind of fun
Enemies caught in a borrowed song
Proving the both of us wrong

subject: an underdog team's final game
lyrics:
[Verse]
Nobody picked us, nobody cared
Walked on the field like we never scared
Bottom of the standings all year long
But tonight we're singing a different song

[Chorus]
Swing for the fences, give it all
Last little inning, last little ball
They wrote us off but here we stand
The underdogs with the upper hand

[Verse]
Coach in the dugout chewing his pen
We've been the punchline time and again
But the scoreboard's close and the crowd's gone wild
Dreaming big like a hopeful child

[Bridge]
Whatever happens when the last out's called
We showed this town we were never small
Grab your glove and grab your friend
We go down swinging till the end

[Chorus]
Swing for the fences, give it all
Last little inning, last little ball
They wrote us off but here we stand
The underdogs with the upper hand

subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
lyrics:
[Verse]
Rain came down on a crowded street
One umbrella, two pairs of feet
You ducked under without a word
Probably the strangest thing I've heard

[Chorus]
Just two strangers in the rain
Sharing shelter, sharing pain
Don't know your name, don't know your town
But you kept my whole world from drowning down

[Verse]
Shoulders touching, we watched it pour
Talked about nothing and a little more
The water rising at our shoes
Two lonely people with nothing to lose

[Bridge]
The storm's gonna pass and you'll walk away
And I'll never see your face again someday
But for these few minutes under the gray
You were the only warm thing in my day

[Chorus]
Just two strangers in the rain
Sharing shelter, sharing pain
Don't know your name, don't know your town
But you kept my whole world from drowning down

subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
lyrics:
[Verse]
Three a.m. and the diesel hums
Drumming on the wheel with my tired thumbs
Dialed your number from a county line
Just to hear you breathing, just to feel you mine

[Chorus]
It's three in the morning, I'm a thousand miles
Living on coffee and your photograph smiles
Say something soft so I'm not alone
Keep me company on the long way home

[Verse]
The kids are sleeping, you whisper low
Tell me about the things I miss and don't know
The leak in the roof, the dog got fleas
Little ordinary miracles like these

[Bridge]
I'd trade every mile of this endless tar
For a porch light glowing wherever you are
The road keeps taking, the road keeps me
But your voice is the place I'm meant to be

[Chorus]
It's three in the morning, I'm a thousand miles
Living on coffee and your photograph smiles
Say something soft so I'm not alone
Keep me company on the long way home

subject: being talked over in every meeting
lyrics:
[Verse]
I raise my hand in the conference glow
Words half-formed and then they go
Swallowed up by a louder voice
Like silence was my only choice

[Chorus]
Talked over, fading out
A quiet drowned by all the shout
I had the answer, I had the key
But no one stopped to look at me

[Verse]
The slides keep clicking, the clock ticks slow
My good idea has nowhere to go
I write it down in a margin small
A thought that never cleared the wall

[Bridge]
Maybe tomorrow I'll speak up first
Before the bigger voices burst
Or maybe I'll just float away
Into the hum of another gray day

[Chorus]
Talked over, fading out
A quiet drowned by all the shout
I had the answer, I had the key
But no one stopped to look at me

subject: losing your faith and finding it again
lyrics:
[Verse]
I burned my Sunday shoes one year
Traded every hymn for fear
The steeple looked like a pointing knife
And I walked out on my own quiet life

[Chorus]
I lost it, lost it, let it go
Drifting where the cold winds blow
Then I heard a horn cut through the night
And something stubborn started to feel right

[Verse]
Danced in the dark for a couple years
Drowning all my doubts in beers
But grace has a funny upstroke beat
It finds you dancing in the street

[Bridge]
Not the same way that I believed before
No gilded ceiling, no marble floor
Just a skank and a horn and a hand to hold
A warmer faith than the one of old

[Chorus]
I found it, found it, took it slow
In the places I didn't think to go
A crooked, joyful, upbeat light
And something stubborn started to feel right

subject: the last day of summer before college
lyrics:
[Verse]
Golden hour on the same old street
Sand still stuck to our tired feet
Tomorrow morning the boxes pack
A road I'm taking that don't lead back

[Chorus]
One more night, let it ride
With all my people by my side
The summer's ending, the future calls
But tonight we own these empty halls

[Verse]
We drove around with the windows low
To every spot that we used to go
Freeze the picture, hold the frame
Nothing's ever gonna feel the same

[Bridge]
We promise calls and we promise more
Knowing somehow it won't be like before
So let me hug you a little too long
Before the morning takes you gone

[Chorus]
One more night, let it ride
With all my people by my side
The summer's ending, the future calls
But tonight we own these empty halls

subject: a panic attack on the subway
lyrics:
[Verse]
Doors slid shut and the lights went tight
A thousand strangers, a tunnel night
My heart took off like a runaway train
Nobody saw the rising strain

[Chorus]
Breathe in slow on the downtown line
Tell my body that I'm gonna be fine
The walls press close but they cannot win
Count the stops and I'll make it in

[Verse]
Sweat on my collar, can't find the air
Clutching a pole like a wordless prayer
The ad above me says find your peace
While my whole chest begs for release

[Bridge]
Then a stranger met my eye and smiled
Said easy now, just rest a while
And the train pulled up to my own stop
And I felt the fever finally drop

[Chorus]
Breathe in slow on the downtown line
Tell my body that I'm gonna be fine
The walls press close but they cannot win
Count the stops and I'll make it in

subject: the last night of a festival
lyrics:
[Verse]
Three days deep in the dust and lights
The wildest of the summer nights
Last set's starting, the bassline drops
Nobody here wants the music to stop

[Chorus]
Last night, last dance, last groove
Everybody in the crowd gonna move
We'll sleep when the sun comes creeping back
But tonight we ride this funky track

[Verse]
Glitter and mud on a stranger's face
Strangers turned family all over the place
The horns kick in and we lose our minds
Leaving every worry behind

[Bridge]
When the speakers cut and the field goes still
We'll carry this fire over every hill
A whole year waiting to feel this free
So give me one more downbeat, set me free

[Chorus]
Last night, last dance, last groove
Everybody in the crowd gonna move
We'll sleep when the sun comes creeping back
But tonight we ride this funky track
